title: Connelly Early once again gave losing Red Sox a chance to win, but failed to pitch deeper into the game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed one run in the Red Sox' 3-2 loss to the Cardinals but struggled with command.
Early's high pitch count due to command issues led to his early exit after 4.1 innings and 86 pitches.
Reliever Zack Kelly gave up the lead in the fifth inning, leading to the Red Sox' 4-9 overall record.

title: It’s been decades since a center won the NBA Most Improved Player award. Will Neemias Queta be next?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Neemias Queta's performance has made him a candidate for the NBA Most Improved Player award.
Queta is averaging 25.5 minutes per game, playing in 75 games with 74 starts this season.
The Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference, with Queta's contributions highlighted.

title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox lineup top batters struggled, managing just one hit against Dustin May, extending Boston's slow start to the season.
Suggestions include moving Wilyer Abreu and Roman Anthony to fourth and fifth in the lineup for better production.
Prospect Mickey Gasper, batting .400 in Worcester, could be called up to potentially boost Red Sox's offense.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics clinched No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference after defeating Pelicans 144-118 at TD Garden.
Boston tied NBA single-game three-point record, making 29 threes for the second time in franchise history.
Celtics to face either Orlando Magic or Philadelphia 76ers in playoffs, pending play-in tournament outcome.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Pizza Movie' featuring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone is now streaming on Hulu.
'Sirat' explores a complex geopolitical event between Morocco and Spain, available on Hulu.
'The Testaments,' a sequel to 'The Handmaid's Tale,' is streaming on Hulu.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum is ruled out for Celtics' game against Pelicans due to right Achilles management.
Jaylen Brown, despite left Achilles tendinitis, will play against the Pelicans after a one-game absence.
Celtics aim to secure the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference with a win against the Pelicans.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter's cancer journey.
Her daughter, Emma, was diagnosed with a medulloblastoma mass in April 2025, requiring immediate surgery and extensive treatment.
Robertson, a former pediatric respiratory therapist, moved to Philadelphia for Emma's proton radiation amid Boston facility maintenance.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Top Bruins prospect James Hagens will not debut against Tampa Bay as Boston pushes to clinch a playoff berth.
Coach Marco Sturm emphasizes patience with 19-year-old Hagens, noting his need to adjust to the team's system first.
Hagens practiced on Boston's fifth line and earned reps on the second power-play unit during Friday's session.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers rank on Forbes' 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' list for their business achievements.
Historic Bostonians like Benjamin Franklin and Babe Ruth appear on Forbes' 'The Greatest Historic Self-Made Americans' list.
Forbes' rankings use a 'Self-Made Score' and draw on archives, expert input, and AI research tools to evaluate candidates.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon will occur on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and finishing at Copley Square.
The race will feature 30,000 participants from over 130 countries, including elite athletes and charity runners.
Spectators can watch the marathon live on WCVB Channel 5 or via the Very Local app starting at 4 a.m.
